WHS Compliance App
Welcome to site
This app manages your daily WHS compliance — sign in, toolbox talks, pre-starts, hazard reporting and more. Setup takes about 2 minutes.
Daily sign in & out
Your attendance is recorded with time and location at the moment you sign in — not tracked at any other time.
Safety forms & records
Toolbox talks, plant pre-starts, hazard reports and incident reports — all in one place, submitted in seconds.
Safety Stars
Earn points for compliance actions and standout safety behaviour. Monthly leaderboard and prize draws.
Your data — This app collects your name, photo, GPS location at sign in/out, and form submissions for site WHS compliance purposes. Your data is stored securely in Australia, managed by Slán Safety on behalf of your employer, and never sold or shared with third parties. Location is recorded only at the moment you sign in or out.

Set up your profile

You only do this once.
After this, signing in takes two taps.

Your details

Site Supervisor

Full dashboard — see all workers, pre-starts, hazards in real time

Worker / Operator

Standard access — sign in, toolbox talk, pre-start, report hazards

Company Director

Company-wide analytics dashboard — full overview across all sites

Specific role / trade
You'll get a reminder 15 minutes before this if pre-start isn't done
Profile photo
Optional — appears in the app header
Licences & tickets
These are sourced from your induction record and training matrix. You will see them in your profile — no need to enter them here.
Signature
Sign with your finger. Stored securely on this device and applied automatically to every form.
Supervisor Access
Enter your supervisor PIN
Incorrect PIN — try again

App permissions

A few permissions are needed to keep you and your team safe.

Why we need these
This app uses your camera, location and notifications to keep you and your team safe on site. Photos and locations are stored on your company's secure systems for compliance and audit purposes, and may be accessed by an external WHS compliance service engaged by your company. By tapping Allow you consent to this use.
View full privacy policy →
Camera
Photos for permits and hazard reports
Tap to allow
Location
Sign in/out, weather, GPS-tagged photos
Tap to allow
Notifications
Permit updates, alerts, supervisor messages
Tap to allow
Privacy Policy

[Company Name] Safety App — Privacy Policy

What we collect

Camera (photos for permits and hazard reports), Location (sign in/out, weather data, GPS-tagged photos), Notifications (alerts only).

How we store it

Data is held on [Company Name]'s secure systems for the duration required by Australian workplace safety law — including audit, insurance, and incident retention requirements.

Who has access
  • Your site supervisor, project director, and authorised company personnel
  • An external WHS compliance service engaged by [Company Name] to manage safety records and reporting on their behalf
  • Regulators (e.g. SafeWork) if required by law or during an investigation
Your rights

You can request to see your data at any time by contacting your supervisor or company. Data is held for compliance and may not be deleted before retention requirements are met.

Contact

[provided by your company]

Toggle simulates a Total Fire Ban for demo. Set to "Force TFB" then open Hot Works permit to see the block. Auto = real check (or default clear if no backend).

You must tick each condition to confirm you have read and will follow it. Then sign at the bottom.

Fitness for Duty Declaration
You must confirm this before signing in
I confirm that I am fit for duty and capable of performing my tasks safely and effectively today. This includes being free from the influence of alcohol, drugs, or any substances that may impair my judgment or performance, and free from the effects of fatigue. If I feel unfit for any reason, I will notify my supervisor immediately before commencing work.
End of Shift Declaration
Please confirm before signing out
If you need to report an injury, incident or hazard, tap Cancel and use the relevant tile on the home screen before signing out.

⚠️ Immediate action first
For serious injuries call 000 first. For fatalities or serious injuries — call your provider before completing this form. Complete this form as soon as the immediate situation is under control.
1 — Incident details
2 — Person(s) involved
3 — Work context
4 — What happened
5 — Immediate action & corrective action
6 — Is this notifiable to the regulator?
Under the WHS Act, certain incidents must be notified to SafeWork / WorkSafe immediately. This includes fatalities, serious injuries requiring hospitalisation, and dangerous incidents (near miss with potential for serious harm).
Stop — call your provider now before continuing. Notifiable incidents require immediate verbal notification to the regulator. Do not disturb the scene. Call your Slán Safety provider immediately.
Declaration
No saved signature — go to Profile
By submitting I confirm this report is accurate to the best of my knowledge. I understand this report will be sent directly to my WHS provider within 24 hours as required under WHS Act 2011.
Confidential reporting
This report goes directly to your WHS provider — not to your site supervisor. You can report anonymously. All reports are handled with strict confidentiality.
Type of concern
Tell us what happened
Your details
Submit anonymously — my name will not be recorded
Anonymous reports are still reviewed and acted upon. Your identity is not required. If not anonymous, your profile name is used — it will not be shared with your employer without your consent unless legally required.

Recent awards received
No awards yet this month — keep up the great work!
🏆
Monthly prizes
Gold tier workers go into the monthly prize draw. Points reset each month — stay consistent!
How points are awarded
Points are awarded two ways — automatically when you complete compliance tasks, and by your supervisor for standout safety behaviour.
Automatic — every action
Sign-ins and pre-starts are mandatory — consistency is rewarded through the weekly streak instead.
Sign toolbox talk15 pts
Report a hazard25 pts
Weekly streak bonuses — Friday
Awarded automatically on Friday if you've hit the target every day that week.
Signed in every day Mon–Fri+25 pts
All pre-starts done Mon–Fri+25 pts
All toolbox talks signed Mon–Fri+25 pts
Supervisor awarded — discretionary
Correct PPE worn without being asked10–50
Hazard spotted and reported25–50
Great housekeeping at end of shift10–25
Helped a new worker or visitor25
Above & beyond safety behaviour50–100
Positive attitude and team culture10–25
Tier thresholds
🥇 Gold500+ points
🥈 Silver250–499 points
🥉 Bronze1–249 points

Company Documents

Insurance certificates, licences and company compliance documents

These documents are stored in your Slán Safety portal and SharePoint. Use this screen to track expiry dates and get alerts before they lapse. Expired insurance means you cannot legally work on most principal contractor sites.

WHS Compliance Certificate
Generate your monthly compliance summary to attach to tenders, prequalification applications, or send to a principal contractor on request.
Take 5

Take 5 Pre-Task Check

Stop and think before you start. 5 questions — 2 minutes.

Complete this before starting any task. If you answer NO to any question, stop work and speak to your supervisor before proceeding.
1
Have I identified all hazards associated with this task?
2
Do I have the right controls in place to manage those hazards?
3
Do I have the right tools, equipment and PPE for this task?
4
Is the area I am working in safe — no uncontrolled hazards from other trades?
5
Am I fit and capable of doing this task safely right now?
STOP WORK
You have answered NO to one or more questions. Do not proceed with this task. Notify your supervisor immediately before commencing any work in this area.
Your Take 5 will be saved and time-stamped. This record is stored in the Slán Safety system as evidence of your pre-task assessment.
⚠ EMERGENCY MUSTER
Mark every person on site as SAFE or MISSING. Do not leave site until all persons are accounted for.
0
On site
0
Accounted for
0
Unaccounted
Tap each worker to mark their status

Contact Slán Safety

Send a message to your WHS provider

Messages are reviewed during business hours. For on-site emergencies call 000. For urgent WHS issues contact your site supervisor first.
Your message
SDS Lookup

Safety Data Sheets

Search for any chemical or product used on this site

🧪
Search for a chemical
Type the product name above to find its Safety Data Sheet. All SDS documents are stored in your company SharePoint — the developer will connect this to your live chemical register.
Chemical not listed?
If a chemical is being used on site that is not in this register, it must be reported immediately. Do not use an unregistered chemical without a current SDS.
Reported - supervisor has been notified
Under WHS Regulations, a current SDS must be accessible for every hazardous chemical on site. SDS must not be older than 5 years from issue. 10 chemicals registered — SLSREG-005 v1.3. 1 expired, 1 expiring in 14 days.
Rigging & Electrical

Rigging & Electrical

Update inspection records for rigging gear and electrical equipment

Current register status
Loading register...
Submit an update
Update type
Item details
Inspection result
Notes (optional)
This update will be saved to Firebase and will feed into your SharePoint rigging & electrical register via the developer integration. A notification will be sent to the Slán Safety portal.
Rigging gear must be inspected before each use and by a licensed rigger every 3 months (AS 4991). Electrical equipment must be tested and tagged every 3 months on construction sites (AS/NZS 3760). Failed items must be removed from service immediately.

Hot Works Permits
Pending approval
Active on site
History (today)
No permits today
Worker-submitted Hot Works permits will appear here for your review.
Review Permit
Conditions for approval
Conditions are extra rules to follow during work. If equipment is missing or setup is wrong, REJECT instead with the reason — workers should fix issues before a fresh permit.
Quick-pick conditions:
Use buddy system
Extra 5m clearance
Extended fire watch (60 min)
Hourly check-in
Stop work if wind >25 km/h
Custom condition (optional):
Reason for rejection *
This permit is currently active. Revoking will immediately invalidate it and notify the worker to stop work.
Reason for revocation *
Review Heights Permit
Conditions for approval
Conditions are extra rules to follow during work. If equipment is missing or rescue plan is not in place, REJECT instead with the reason — workers should fix issues before a fresh permit.
Quick-pick conditions:
Stop if wind >25 km/h
Dedicated spotter
100% tie-off
Hourly check-in
Brief rescue plan
Monitor exclusion zone
Beat forecast weather
Custom condition (optional):
Reason for rejection *
Acknowledge Conditions
⚠ Approval with conditions
Your supervisor approved permit — with conditions. You must acknowledge each condition before work can commence.
I have read and accept all conditions above. I will follow them for the duration of this permit.
Close Heights Permit
Closeout declaration
Tick each item below to confirm the work area has been left safe before closing this permit.
Comments (optional)
Cannot Sign Out
You have active Hot Works permits
You must close each active permit before signing out of site. Tap a permit below to complete its closeout declaration.
Close Permit
Closeout checks
Comments (optional)
Fire Watch Handover
⚠ Why this matters
AS 1674.1 requires the fire watch must continue for the full duration after work ends. If you need to leave site before it's complete, a competent person on site must take over. Their name is logged on this permit.
Hand over to *
⚠ No supervisors are currently signed in to site. You cannot hand over right now — call your supervisor to come on site, or wait for the fire watch to complete.
Photo of cooled work area *
Show the area where hot works took place. This proves no smouldering at handover.
I confirm the work area is cooled, no smouldering observed, and fire extinguisher remains in place. The named supervisor will be asked to accept this handover on their device before I can sign out.
Waiting for Supervisor

Hot Works
Hot Works Permit

Hot Works Permit

Fill in all fields — submit when complete

Permit Identification
Applicant
Work Details
Work Times
Maximum 12 hours per AS 1674.1 (one-shift max). End time will be capped automatically.
Weather Conditions
Pre-Start Safety Checks
All items must be confirmed before the permit can be submitted.
Combustible materials removed or protected within 10m
Floor / ground swept clean of dust and debris
Drains, openings and penetrations covered
Gas lines and flammable services isolated (if applicable)
Fire extinguisher on site, in date, within 5m
Fire blanket available (where required)
PPE confirmed — gloves, helmet, eye / face protection
Ventilation adequate for the task
Adjacent workers notified
Gas cylinders secured upright and >3m from ignition source (if applicable)
Fire Watch
Fire watch required for this task
Emergency
Photo evidence

Take photos of the work area and setup. Photos optional for now (camera issue being fixed). You can submit without them.

Confirmation & Sign-off
I confirm all information is accurate and I have read the site SWMS for this task

Working at Heights

Heights Permit

Working at Heights Permit

Fill in all fields — submit when complete

Permit Identification
Applicant
Work Details *
A permit is required for any work over 2m where a fall could occur.
Fall Prevention & Equipment *
Work Times *
Maximum 12 hours per one-shift safe-work practice. End time will be capped automatically.
Conditions & Site Setup *
Stop work if wind exceeds 30 km/h (or as per equipment manufacturer's limit).
View company rescue plan template (PDF)
Pre-start Safety Checks *
Anchor points rated, certified and pre-use inspected
Harness in date, undamaged, fitted correctly
Lanyard / SRL in date, free running, no damage
Edge protection / guardrails in place where required
Exclusion zone below set up with barriers and signage
Weather acceptable (wind <30 km/h, no rain on slippery surfaces)
Rescue plan in place and communicated to all involved
Spotter / observer assigned (especially if working alone)
Tools tethered or area below cleared of personnel
Working surface inspected (no holes, fragile material identified)
Photo Evidence
Tap each box to take a photo. Photos are time-stamped and GPS-tagged automatically.
Sign-off
I confirm all of the above is correct and I will follow all safe-work practices for this permit.

Override TFB & Approve
Total Fire Ban active
Hot works can only proceed under a valid Fire Service TFB Permit. Complete all fields below to approve with override.
Fire Service authorisation
Additional TFB-day safety measures
Beyond standard extinguisher — e.g. water source within 10m, additional extinguishers, fire hose connected, fire blanket
⚠ Override will be recorded on the permit audit trail with your name, signature, and timestamp. Worker must acknowledge all extra TFB conditions before starting work.
Total Fire Ban Active
Hot works are not permitted in your district today (State Fire Service).
Your permit will be submitted to your supervisor with a TFB flag.
🔥 TFB Test Toggle
Simulate a Total Fire Ban for testing. Set to "Force TFB" then submit a Hot Works permit to see the block.
Currently: auto
